step_2.name: Function Replacement
step_2.old_function: run_claude_scene_builder(episode_num: int, srt_content: str, duration_sec: float) -> dict
step_2.new_function: run_claude_scene_builder(episode_num: int, script_path: str, srt_path: str, duration_sec: float) -> list
changes: Added script_path parameter for direct access to episode script
Added srt_path parameter for direct file access (instead of pre-parsed content)
Returns type changed from dict to list (array of scene objects)
New implementation writes context files to /home/agentuser/remotion/ for Claude Code to read
Claude Code now runs in REMOTION_DIR with full file access to src/scenes/ and src/Root.jsx
Claude can now CREATE new Remotion scene components when existing library is insufficient
Claude validates new scenes with test render calls (npx remotion render)
Full prompt rewritten for comprehensive scene analysis and building capability
Timeout increased from 120s to 1200s (20 minutes) for complex builds
Max turns set to 30 for iterative scene development
Output validation checks that ep{episode_num}_scene_sequence.json exists and is valid JSON array
Context files cleaned up after completion (no temp file leaks)

changes.reason: Removed hardcoded token; now reads from TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var first, fallback to INFRA_BOT_TOKEN, finally empty string
Reads from TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID env var first, fallback to REINIER_CHAT_ID, finally hardcoded ID

step_4.name: Caption Burn Investigation
findings: Uses ASS subtitle format (ass= filter in ffmpeg) at line 445
Called once in main() at line 501
No duplication detected: function is called exactly once with correct parameters
muxed_mp4 (video+audio) is passed as input, captions are burned via ffmpeg -vf filter
Output written to canonical location via output_path parameter
No evidence of double-burning or audio mixing issues

step_5.name: Syntax Validation
step_5.result: SYNTAX OK - no Python parsing errors

function_call_fix.reason: Updated to match new function signature requiring script_path and srt_path instead of pre-parsed srt_content

key_improvements.claude_code_integration: Claude Code now has full file access to Remotion project, can read scripts and SRT, analyze content deeply, build new scene components when needed
key_improvements.iterative_building: 30-turn max allows Claude to create scenes, test them with Manim, iterate until quality is high
key_improvements.longer_timeout: 20 minutes allows complex builds (creating multiple new scenes, testing, registration)
key_improvements.less_brittle: No longer trying to parse Claude response as JSON; Claude writes file directly, we just validate it exists
key_improvements.context_files: Script and SRT written to disk for Claude to read, enabling line-by-line analysis and timestamp-based scene mapping
key_improvements.guard_logic: Validates output file exists, is valid JSON array, has expected structure before returning

backward_compatibility: Function signature changed - caller must now pass script_path and srt_path instead of srt_content. This is intentional: direct file access gives Claude more control and clarity.

testing.expected: Claude Code will spawn, read EP01 script and SRT, analyze content, build appropriate scene sequence, output ep1_scene_sequence.json
